methods.description: Collection and analysis of proxy records with high temporal resolution in the Holocene across the Afro-Asia region to detect wetting or drying trends.

steps.description: Proxy data included cave and marine core oxygen isotopes, which were measured for precipitation and hydroclimate and downloaded from the paleoclimatology data website of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
steps.searchable_summary: download proxy data from NOAA paleoclimatology website

steps.description: We detected a wetting or drying trend during 2000 consecutive years, as identified by 1000-year averaged oxygen isotopes between a given reference time.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate wetting or drying trends using 1000-year averaged oxygen isotopes

steps.description: The trend throughout 5 ka BP was calculated as the average of 4-5 ka BP minus the average of 5-6 ka BP.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate specific trend for 5 ka BP using average differences

steps.description: We further calculated the standard deviation by using available data of a proxy record in 11-0 ka BP.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate standard deviation of proxy records from 11-0 ka BP

steps.description: Only the trends with a difference greater than 0.3-times the standard deviation for 2000 consecutive years are shown in Fig. 1.
steps.searchable_summary: filter trends with significant difference greater than 0.3-times standard deviation

methods.description: Simulations using the Community Atmospheric Model (CAM) and slab ocean model (SOM) to explore atmospheric response to orbital changes during the Holocene.

steps.description: We used the Community Atmospheric Model (CAM) version 5.1 of the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) and the slab ocean model (SOM).
steps.searchable_summary: set up CAM and SOM models for simulation

steps.description: CAM and SOM simulations were integrated using the finite volume dynamical core with 1°horizontal resolution and 30 vertical levels in the CAM.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate CAM and SOM simulations with specified resolution and levels

steps.description: A total of 21 time-slicing experimental simulations were performed; in addition to the 13 simulations from 11 ka BP to 1 ka AP with interval of 1000 years, extra eight simulations at specific orbital conditions were performed for higher resolution in the late Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: perform time-slicing experimental simulations with specified intervals and conditions

steps.description: The boundary and initial conditions of the simulations were taken from the Community Earth System Model (CESM) preindustrial control experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: set boundary and initial conditions from CESM preindustrial control experiment

steps.description: The model was integrated for 40 years, and outputs for years 21-40 were used to form the climatology.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate model for 40 years and use outputs from years 21-40 for climatology
